title: What Production RAG Actually Demands
excerpt: Demos forgive hallucination. Relationship managers and ticket queues do not. Notes from shipping grounded chat at work.
content:
# What Production RAG Actually Demands

I have shipped RAG in places where a wrong answer is not a shrug — wealth questions for relationship managers, and document chat inside ticketing.

## Grounding beats eloquence

If the retrieved chunks are weak, no prompt saves you. FAISS, clean chunking, and category filters did more for trust than clever wording.

## Operators need a panel

Admin surfaces for documents and categories are not extras. They are how the system stays honest after launch.

## Feedback is a feature

At L&T we treated user feedback as part of the loop for a fine-tuned 7B model. Models drift; processes should not.

## Closing

Production RAG is backend engineering with a retrieval conscience.

title: Trails Teach the Same Discipline as Deploys
excerpt: Kalavantin’s steps and a failing deploy share a lesson: panic makes you slip. Pace is a skill.
content:
# Trails Teach the Same Discipline as Deploys

On Kalavantin Durga the last metres are rock-cut and exposed. You do not rush a foothold to impress anyone.

## Turn-back judgement

Kedarnath weather and production incidents both reward the humility to pause.

## Eleven shrines, one remaining

The Jyotirlinga yatra left Rameswaram ahead on purpose. Unfinished work can be sacred — in code and on the map.

## Closing

I want systems that stay calm. Trails are where I practice that before Monday.
